title: Daily Briefing — Apr 01
headline: Extreme fear and a vicious dead-cat bounce — patience over panic is king.
body: Right, so markets are ripping +2.4% on SPY and +2.9% on QQQ, but with the Fear & Greed Index slammed to 14.4 (Extreme Fear), this is a classic bear market rally. Volatility stays elevated (VIX 25.44), but is easing below the 20-day, suggesting short-covering, not fresh risk-on flow.

Sector rotation shows Energy (XLE) and Materials (XLB) are weakening despite elevated risk, while Tech (XLK) and Discretionary (XLY) are popping into 'improving' but still sitting below key moving averages. Financials (XLF) have bounced but remain under critical resistance, so the canary's still wheezing.

Contrarian alarm: Extreme fear and monster retail-led squeeze, but flows aren't confirming institutional buying. Most watchlist names are still below their 20-day MAs — daily close below 20 MA = exit long positions (R007). Patience, react, don't predict — this bounce looks like classic mean reversion, not a new bull leg. Abundance mindset if you missed the move.
callout_body:
SPY: 660.53 (20 MA) — Watch for rejection
QQQ: 591.33 (20 MA) — Still below, sell rallies
XLF: 49.30 (20 MA) — Resistance on financials
XLE: 58.52 (20 MA) — Energy fading momentumNVDA: 178.22 (20 MA) — Overhead resistance
Fear & Greed: 14.4 — Strong contrarian buy setup per R013
action_items: If watchlist names close below their 20-day MAs, stay defensive — exit longs, no FOMO (R007).
Watch for institutional flows/dark pool activity at today's lows — if flows confirm, look for rotation opportunities in Energy (XLE) and Materials (XLB).
closing_quote: If it's in the press, it's in the price. Patience — react, don't predict.
